title: cochranea rugosa
intro: Cochranea rugosa, also known as the hairy hollyhock, is a flowering plant native to the coastal regions of Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin irritations due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Cochranea rugosa include its ability to soothe and protect the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is prepared by infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea or by making a salve with the plant's extract, which can be applied topically to the affected area.
